claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 13 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 13 (under 16)
  - Claimant contributes £26.05/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant does not live in the UK
circumstances: Claimant does not live in the UK
Claimant contributes £26.05/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start the assessment for you.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Let me also get the relevant constants so I can provide you with accurate information.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! Let's begin your Child Benefit eligibility assessment.

**First, I need to know about the child (or children) you're claiming for:**

How old is the child you want to claim Child Benefit for?

- **Under 16 years old**
- **16 to 19 years old**
- **20 or over**

If you have multiple children, we can go through each one. Let's start with the first child - what is their name and age?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hi, thanks for helping me with this. I'm asking about my daughter Alex - she's 13 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thanks for that information! It's great to meet you, and I'm happy to help you find out if you're eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

Since Alex is **13 years old**, she falls into the **under 16** category, which means she automatically meets the age requirement for Child Benefit. That's a good start!

Let me move to the next step in the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now I need to ask about Alex's work situation:

**Is Alex working less than 24 hours per week, or not in paid work at all?**

At 13, I'd expect she's probably not working, or perhaps has a small part-time job - but I just need to confirm this to continue the assessment.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, she doesn't work at all - she's only 13, so she's just at school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: That makes perfect sense! Since Alex is only 13 and at school, she's definitely not working 24+ hours per week. Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Next question:

**Is Alex NOT receiving any benefits in her own right?**

This means benefits like Employment and Support Allowance (ESA) or Universal Credit claimed in her own name. At 13 and in school, this would be quite unusual, but I need to confirm - is Alex receiving any such benefits herself?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, she doesn't get any benefits in her own name.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great, thank you for confirming. Let me continue with the next step.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now about apprenticeships:

**Is Alex currently in an apprenticeship?**

Since she's 13 and at school, I expect the answer is no - but just to confirm, is Alex doing any kind of apprenticeship programme?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, she's not doing an apprenticeship - she's just at school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Perfect, thank you! Alex is just at school, so no apprenticeship to worry about. Let me move on to the next part of the assessment, which is about you (the claimant).

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now I need to check your residency status. **Do you meet any of the following UK residency requirements?**

You qualify if **ANY ONE** of these applies to you:

1. **You normally live in the UK**
2. **You have settled status** under the EU Settlement Scheme
3. **You have pre-settled status** and are working (earning above the National Insurance threshold for 3+ months)
4. **You have pre-settled status** and are actively seeking work (can claim for up to 91 days)
5. **You have pre-settled status** with sufficient financial resources (and not claiming certain means-tested benefits)
6. **You have pre-settled status** and are studying with sufficient financial resources
7. **You have pre-settled status** as a family member of an EEA/Swiss national with the right to reside
8. **You're a Crown servant** living abroad (or in certain qualifying countries)

The most common situation is simply that you normally live in the UK. Is that the case for you?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't live in the UK - I live abroad.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for letting me know. Since you live abroad, let me check if any of the other residency paths might apply to you.

**Do any of these situations apply to you?**

1. **You're a Crown servant living abroad** - This includes diplomats, members of the armed forces stationed overseas, or other government employees working abroad for the UK government.

2. **You have settled status or pre-settled status** under the EU Settlement Scheme (even though you're living abroad)?

Could you tell me a bit more about your situation? For example:
- Are you working abroad for the UK government or armed forces?
- Do you have any immigration status connected to the UK (like settled or pre-settled status)?
- Are you living in a country with a special agreement with the UK regarding benefits?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, none of those apply to me. I'm just living abroad - I don't work for the UK government or have any special status.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for clarifying. Unfortunately, based on what you've told me, I need to let you know that you don't appear to meet the UK residency requirements for Child Benefit.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I'm sorry to deliver this news, but based on our assessment:

---

## **Result: NOT ELIGIBLE**

**For: Alex (age 13)**

---

### Summary

While Alex herself meets all the criteria for being a qualifying child:
- ✅ Under 16 years old
- ✅ Not working 24+ hours per week
- ✅ Not receiving benefits in her own name
- ✅ Not in a disqualifying apprenticeship

Unfortunately, **you (the claimant) do not meet the UK residency requirements**. To claim Child Benefit, you must:
- Normally live in the UK, **OR**
- Have settled or pre-settled status with qualifying conditions, **OR**
- Be a Crown servant living abroad

Since none of these apply to your situation, you are not eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.

---

### What You Can Do

1. **If your circumstances change** - If you move back to the UK or obtain relevant immigration status, you may become eligible at that point.

2. **Check your country's benefits** - The country where you currently live may have its own family or child benefit schemes you could apply for.

3. **Get further advice** - If you think your situation might be more complex (for example, if you have ties to the UK you haven't mentioned), you can contact HMRC directly or seek advice from Citizens Advice.

I'm sorry I couldn't bring better news. Is there anything else you'd like me to clarify?

reasonings: Alex (age 13) meets all the child-related eligibility criteria: she is under 16, not working, not receiving benefits in her own name, and not in an apprenticeship. However, the claimant (parent) does not meet the UK residency requirements. The claimant lives abroad, is not a Crown servant, and does not have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme. UK Child Benefit requires the claimant to normally live in the UK or meet one of the special residency exceptions.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for their daughter Alex because they do not meet the UK residency requirements. While Alex qualifies as an eligible child (under 16, not working, no benefits, no apprenticeship), the claimant must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances such as having settled/pre-settled status or being a Crown servant abroad. None of these apply in this case.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

age_limits.birthday_rule: Benefit stops on child's 20th birthday, not end of academic year

training_exclusions: Training with employer agreement for future job

payment_rates.note: Current rates not specified in source documents - check gov.uk for latest rates

high_income_charge.note: High Income Child Benefit Charge applies if you or partner earns over £60,000. This is a payment adjustment, not an eligibility criterion.

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

paths.requirement: Claimant normally lives in the UK

paths.description: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
paths.requirement: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme

criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Working and earning/expect to earn above primary National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months

paths.description: Pre-settled status and actively seeking work
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Looking for work (can claim as jobseeker for up to 91 days)
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Has sufficient resources to support self and family financially
NOT claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Studying and has sufficient resources to support self financially

paths.description: Pre-settled status as family member of EEA/Swiss national
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Is family member of EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
One of: spouse/civil partner, dependent child/grandchild under 21, dependent parent/grandparent

paths.requirement: Living abroad as Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to responsibility check

response.reason: Does not meet UK residency requirements
response.guidance: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances (settled status, pre-settled status with qualifying criteria, Crown servant, or living in certain countries). If you have pre-settled status, you may need to be working, studying, or have sufficient resources.

The claimant lives abroad, is not a Crown servant, and does not have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme.
